content: The orthographic variant 'reflexion' (the older, less common spelling of reflection in English language education) can be repurposed as a label for a tiered LLM self-reflection mechanism: 'reflexion' refers to the fast, implicit, memory-based first-pass self-check of an LLM's output, while the standard spelling 'reflection' refers to the slow, deliberate, multi-perspective structured self-critique that aligns with existing LLM self-reflection frameworks. This split leverages the dual meaning of the term across language education and AI agent research to create a natural categorization of two distinct self-reflection modes.
